prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh sweetheart, well, let me see if I can explain this the way your Uncle Dave explained it to me! You know how our whole house needs a brain to keep everything running - someone to tell the lights when to turn on and make sure the stove knows what temperature to be? Well, the CPU is sort of like the brain of the computer, it does all the thinking and figures out what needs to happen next. Every time you click something or type a letter, that little CPU is working away in there, quick as a wink, figuring out what to do. I don't pretend to understand all the ins and outs of it myself, but the nice man at the computer store once told me that a better CPU means the computer can think faster. So basically, honey, it's just the little fellow inside the machine that keeps everything going - isn't that something!
